merged_findings: Features e8 through e12 demonstrate strong class-conditioned structural variation, with BENIGN class showing higher variance ratios and unique value counts compared to DDoS, particularly in backward packet length features (e9-e12).

Features e13 through e22 exhibit strong dependency concentration with near-perfect linear correlations, indicating structural redundancy that aligns with and likely reflects the class-conditioned variation observed in e8-e12, suggesting an interplay between dependency structure and class-conditioned variation.

Moderate label neighborhood conflicts and duplicate label conflicts in backward packet length features, along with shortcut-like predictive signals (e.g., Bwd Packet Length Max), reveal unstable feature importance and entanglement between class-conditioned variation and redundancy, contributing to selection bias.
open_gaps: Direct distribution verification for features e11 and e12 was not performed due to step budget exhaustion, though strong locality and shared group context support inferred similarity.
Analyses are limited to local dependency clusters and single-feature shortcuts without global aggregation or cross-feature group synthesis, leaving broader structural interplay unresolved.
update_focus: Further investigation should target global aggregation of dependency structures and cross-feature group synthesis to clarify the full extent of interplay between class-conditioned variation and dependency redundancy, while addressing unstable feature importance and shortcut effects in backward packet length features.
